steps.title: Set up the unit rate equation
steps.content:
The relationship between total cost, price per unit, and quantity is:
$$\text{Total cost} = \text{Price per pound} \times \text{Number of pounds}$$
Substituting the given information:
$$20 = 4p$$
where $p$ is the number of pounds, 20 is the total cost, and 4 is the price per pound.

steps.title: Solve for the number of pounds
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 4:
$$p = \frac{20}{4} = 5$$
The shopper purchased 5 pounds of pears.
